✨How to Make a Bed the Clean Mama Way

Step 1: Straighten your fitted sheet
Give it a quick tug to make sure it’s smooth and tucked in at the corners. This is the foundation — if it’s lumpy, the whole bed looks messy.

Step 2: Lay your duvet flat
Shake it out, center it on the bed, and let it fall evenly on both sides. No tucking needed.

Step 3: Fold the top down
Fold the top edge of the duvet down about 10–12 inches. This little detail makes a bed look intentional and hotel-like without any extra effort.

Step 4: Arrange your pillows
Place your sleeping pillows flat first, then lean any accent pillows against them. I keep it to two sleeping pillows plus one extra — anything more and you’re just making more work for yourself every single night.

Step 5: Smooth and done
One quick pass with your hands across the whole surface. Add a throw blanket to the bottom if you want a little extra or if you use one. That’s it. Step back and admire it. 

Three minutes. Every morning. Life-changing? Genuinely, yes.

✨Comment BED for a link to my full bed-making method — including the bedding formula that makes this so fast and links to my bedding favorites.

Take the guesswork out of what to clean and when to clean it.

The FREE June Homekeeping Calendar includes the Daily and Weekly Tasks—the foundation of the Clean Mama Routine.

These simple tasks help you: ✔️ Keep your home functioning ✔️ Prevent mess and buildup ✔️ Eliminate daily decision fatigue ✔️ Stay consistent without starting over

Try it for one week and notice how much calmer your home feels.

This came up in a recent Homekeeping Society Q&A and I knew I had to share it here too.

Most cleaning routines fail because there’s no built-in reset.
That’s why I built Friday Catch-All Day into the Clean Mama Routine. 
Every Friday I:  ✔️ tie up loose ends  ✔️ reset the kitchen  ✔️ make a meal plan  ✔️ clean out the fridge  ✔️ catch up on laundry  ✔️ prep for the weekend 
Not perfectly. Not all day long. Just enough to stop the house from spiraling.
Friday Catch-All changed everything for me — I stopped spending my entire weekend cleaning.
